fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer pharmacist license to georgia, pharmacist compact georgia, georgia pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Georgia Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Professional Licensing Boards Division
237 Coliseum Drive
Macon, Georgia 31217-3858

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Georgia.

steps.title: Register with NABP and Request Score Transfers
steps.description: Create an NABP e-Profile, apply for the NAPLEX and MPJE exams, and ensure your scores are requested to be transferred to the Georgia State Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Create an Online Licensing Account
steps.description: Visit the Georgia Online Licensing System and register as a new user to create your account and obtain a user ID.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into the Georgia Online Licensing System, select 'Apply for a New License', complete the pharmacist application for either examination or endorsement, and pay the required fee.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official pharmacy school transcripts to be sent directly from your accredited institution to the Georgia State Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Background Check
steps.description: Register for and complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through the Georgia Applicant Processing Services (GAPS) as required by the Board.

steps.title: Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Successfully pass both the NAPLEX and MPJE examinations, if applying by examination, with results reported directly to the Georgia Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Georgia. To work in Georgia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Georgia
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Georgia, all initial applications, including licensure by examination and endorsement, must be submitted through the Georgia Online Licensing System.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Georgia Online Licensing System, click 'Register a Person', and follow the prompts to create an account and user ID. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License' under the 'Initial Application' section.
